phase_name: Sessions 33-34: RH Attack via Connes Q_W Positivity
phase_dir: N/A - research sessions, not GSD phase

completed_tasks.name: Direction A: Sieve bypass for Connes eps_0 > 0

remaining_tasks.name: Prove M_diag dominates M_prime on orth(Poisson kernel)
Closed-form M_diag eigenvalue bounds via digamma asymptotics
PNT/sieve bound on M_prime restricted to orth(v_+)
Formalize 2x2 range proof in Lean with full Abel summation

blockers.description: All analytic bounds (sieve, trace-norm, Gershgorin, concentration) are circular for full null block proof
blockers.workaround: The M_diag domination approach may bypass this — M_diag is analytic (no primes), M_prime is bounded by PNT

blockers.description: Hard Lefschetz in characteristic one is an open problem in algebraic geometry
blockers.workaround: The M_diag domination approach is a purely analytic alternative that avoids geometry

decisions.decision: Range/null split of Q_W as primary attack
decisions.rationale: W02 has rank 2, splitting reduces dim-dimensional problem to 2x2 (proved) + (dim-2) null block

decisions.decision: M has signature (1, dim-1) — one positive eigenvalue
decisions.rationale: Computed at all tested lambda. The positive eigenvector is the Poisson kernel, 99.98% aligned with W02

decisions.decision: Negativity comes from M_diag dominating M_prime, not prime cancellation
decisions.rationale: Individual T_minor(p^k) are ALL positive on orth(v_+). M_diag trace -65 vs M_prime op norm 1.75

decisions.decision: Hodge-Riemann sign convention resolves: Q_W > 0 on primitive = Hodge index holds
decisions.rationale: Q_W = -(intersection form), sign flip from dim 2 arithmetic surface

next_action: Prove M_diag dominates M_prime on orth(Poisson kernel). M_diag has closed-form entries (digamma). Compute its eigenvalues analytically. Bound M_prime op norm on orth(v_+) using PNT. The 37x trace margin suggests per-eigenvalue domination should close.
context_notes: Sessions 33-34 were a sustained attack on RH via Connes Q_W positivity. 24 commits, ~20K lines. Every analytic approach for the full null block is circular — the explicit formula identity makes M<=0 equivalent to RH. BUT: the discovery that negativity comes from M_diag (analytic, no primes) dominating M_prime (prime sum) opens a new path. M_diag eigenvalues are computable special functions. M_prime is bounded. If per-eigenvalue domination can be shown, the null block is proved without circularity. This is the most promising remaining path. Grok validated the structural findings and suggested major/minor arc (which revealed the M_diag domination mechanism). 12 Lean files building clean, 5+ proved theorems using Mathlib PSD infrastructure.
